title: The Slingshot
description: A massive body sits just off the course. A gravity assist could bank years of fuel — if the approach is flawless.

outcomes.label: Thread the slingshot
outcomes.description: Fly close, gain speed, risk the tides.
outcomes.log: The ship whipped past the giant and came out faster and lighter on fuel. The tides only rattled the crockery.

outcomes.label: Skip the maneuver
outcomes.description: Burn the fuel, keep the plating.
outcomes.log: The council skipped the slingshot and paid in fuel for the peace of mind.

title: The Prodigy
description: A child in the new cohort reads the ship's systems the way others read faces. The council can fast-track them, or let them grow up ordinary.

outcomes.label: Fast-track the prodigy
outcomes.description: Give them every system and every hour.
outcomes.log: The prodigy was given the run of the ship's systems. In a decade the ship will be glad; the cohort is less sure now.

outcomes.label: Let them be a child
outcomes.description: No cohort should carry the ship on one set of shoulders.
outcomes.log: The council let the prodigy grow up ordinary. The ship kept its gift in reserve, and its childhood intact.

title: The Ion Reef
description: A shoal of charged plasma glitters across the bow, beautiful and unmapped. The instruments want a closer look; the pilots want a wide berth.

outcomes.label: Chart it close
outcomes.description: Thread the shoal for the data.
outcomes.log: A week in the glow, and the survey banks a reef no chart has ever held.

outcomes.label: Skirt it wide
outcomes.description: Log it and keep the distance.
outcomes.log: The reef fell astern, unentered. The pilots slept easier for it.

title: The Clock Disagreement
description: Two chronometers, once synchronized on the founding day, no longer agree. The dark has been bending time by fractions no one felt passing.

outcomes.label: Recalibrate to the beacon
outcomes.description: Trust the outside clock.
outcomes.log: The ship reset its calendar against the last known beacon. A handful of days simply vanished from the record.

outcomes.label: Keep ship-time
outcomes.description: The ship's own year is the true one now.
outcomes.log: The council ruled that ship-time is the only time that matters out here. The old calendar became a story.

title: The Dark Body
description: Something crosses the star-field ahead — a mass that gives back no light, only a shadow where the stars should be.

outcomes.label: Range it with active scan
outcomes.description: Paint it and learn what it is.
outcomes.log: The pulse came back strange and slow. Whatever it is, it is old, and it is not natural.

outcomes.label: Run dark past it
outcomes.description: Give it nothing to look back at.
outcomes.log: The ship ran silent until the shadow was well astern. Some questions keep.

title: The Whisper
description: A faint, patient tug on the ship's mass — a gravity that has no visible source. The astronomers argue in the corridors; the priests have already decided.

outcomes.label: Study the whisper
outcomes.description: Turn every instrument on it.
outcomes.log: Months of data, and only deeper questions. The whisper stayed a whisper — but the ship's scholars will chase it for generations.

outcomes.label: Look away
outcomes.description: Some things are not for us to weigh.
outcomes.log: The instruments were turned elsewhere. The whisper went on tugging, unmeasured.

title: The Slow Pocket
description: Sensors find a fold of deep-slowed time across the course. Cut through it to save fuel, or route the long way around at cost.

outcomes.label: Cut through the fold
outcomes.description: Save the fuel. Let the years outside run strange.
outcomes.log: The ship slid through the slow pocket. To the crew it was a week; the star-charts outside had aged a decade, and the people came out subtly changed.

outcomes.label: Route around it
outcomes.description: Burn the extra fuel for a clean, ordinary passage.
outcomes.log: The navigators gave the fold a wide berth. The fuel gauges dropped and the calendar stayed honest.

title: The Second Star
description: A star that is not on any chart brightens dead ahead — young, wrong, and radiating on frequencies that make the physicists argue and the navigators nervous.

outcomes.label: Hold and study it
outcomes.description: Spend the time. A thing like this may never be seen again.
outcomes.log: The ship held station and drank the strange star in through every instrument. The data would feed the archives for centuries; the schedule never quite recovered the lost years.

outcomes.label: Give it a wide berth
outcomes.description: Whatever it is, it is not the mission. Steer clear.
outcomes.log: The navigators bent the course well around the wrong young star and its restless light. The physicists filed complaints; the captain filed them away.

title: The Wandering Comet
description: A comet on a wild hyperbolic path will cross the ship's course exactly once, trailing volatiles and stranger compounds no survey has catalogued. It will not pass this way again in a hundred lifetimes.

outcomes.label: Match course and sample it
outcomes.description: Burn fuel to ride alongside and harvest what it carries.
outcomes.log: The ship chased the wandering comet down and rode its tail for a season, harvesting ice and rarer things. The tanks ran lower and the holds ran richer, and the comet fled on into the dark forever.

outcomes.label: Let it pass
outcomes.description: Log its path and keep the fuel for the mission.
outcomes.log: The astronomers tracked the comet's once-in-a-hundred-lifetimes pass and let it go untouched. Its data filled a page; its ices filled no hold.

title: The Far Deep
description: The second anomaly is stranger than the first — and stranger than the charter promised. The survey teams argue whether what they are reading is a natural wonder or something that was made.

outcomes.label: Survey it to the bottom
outcomes.description: Spend the crew and the time to read the far deep entire.
outcomes.log: The teams read the far deep down to its strange foundations and lost a few of their number to its stranger depths. What they brought back would occupy the archives for centuries.

outcomes.label: Log the essentials and bank it
outcomes.description: Take the contracted readings and no more risk.
outcomes.log: The survey took its contracted readings of the far deep and asked no further questions of it. The charter was satisfied; the mystery was left, mostly, alone.

title: The Answer
description: Generations ago the ship painted an ancient dark mass with an active pulse — a thing old and not natural, left well astern. Now the long array wakes the watch: a signal has arrived on the exact frequency of that pulse, from dead ahead, weaker and slower and changed, as though the ship's own question has been all this time coming back an answer.

outcomes.label: Answer it
outcomes.description: The ship asked, once. Something answered. Reply, and learn what asks back across the dark.
outcomes.log: The ship replied to the thing that had replied to it, and spent a generation listening to the slow exchange sharpen — question, answer, question — with something that did not think in years. What was learned was worth a fortune to the archives and cost the crew their sleep, and the dark body passed into the ship's oldest ghost stories, verified.

outcomes.label: Never on that frequency again
outcomes.description: The founders should never have pinged it. Strike the frequency from the arrays, seal the old scan, and let the answer go unanswered forever.
outcomes.log: The council forbade the frequency, sealed the founders' scan under the oldest lock the archive had, and let the slow answer arrive to a ship that would not hear it. Whatever had been reaching back reached into silence, and the watch that made the choice slept better for a thing it chose not to know.

title: The Mirror
description: The forward array returns something impossible: a perfect echo of the ship itself, dead ahead and closing — same mass, same drive-signature, same running lights, as if an identical vessel were bearing down on a head-on course. The navigators are fairly sure it is the ship's own emissions, folded back by something massive between here and there. Fairly sure.

outcomes.label: Hail it
outcomes.description: Whatever is folding the ship's light back is worth understanding. Open a channel to the thing wearing the ship's face.
outcomes.log: The ship hailed its own reflection and mapped the lens that made it — a knot of bent space the charts had missed, worth every watt to have measured. For a week the crew watched their own hail come back a heartbeat late, mouthing their own words, and were glad when the lens fell astern.

outcomes.label: Log it and pass
outcomes.description: It is a lensing artifact and nothing more. Note the phenomenon, hold the course, and do not stare at it.
outcomes.log: The navigators logged the mirror as a gravitational artifact, held their heading straight through the ghost of their own ship, and did not dwell on how, for the span of the crossing, something ahead had matched them turn for turn.

title: The Star's Appointed Hour
description: The founders' astronomers computed this year to the season, generations before the ship was built: the dying star will throw its great convulsion now, a once-in-an-age unshrouding of its heart, and the whole charter was written to place the ship in its light when it did. The prediction has held. The star is stirring exactly on the schedule the dead laid down, and the ship rides its atmosphere with the appointed hour upon it.

outcomes.label: Ride it out in close
outcomes.description: This is the hour the voyage was launched for. Hold the ship in tight and take the full reading — the star's bared heart, a harvest of energy and knowledge no one will have another chance at for an age — and pay whatever the closeness costs in hull and in lives.
outcomes.log: The ship held its place in the star's bared light through the appointed hour and took everything the founders had sent it for — a flood of energy, a harvest of knowledge, a look into a stellar heart no living eye had seen. The hull came away scorched and the memorial wall the longer for it, and the founders' three-century wager was paid in full, in both the coin they'd hoped for and the coin they'd feared.

outcomes.label: Pull back and read it from range
outcomes.description: The founders wagered lives they would never have to spend. Honour the mission but not the recklessness: pull to a survivable distance, take the reading the ship can take without burning for it, and bring the crew home to spend the knowledge rather than dying for the last of it.
outcomes.log: The ship pulled back to a distance it could survive and read the star's convulsion from range — a lesser harvest than the founders dreamed, but taken by a crew that lived to carry it home. The dead had wagered the living's lives on the closeness; the living, weighing the same star, decided the founders had booked a debt that was theirs to refuse.

title: The Watchers Answer
description: Generations ago the ship broke the silence and hailed the intelligence that had been watching it. Now, at last, the watchers answer — and the answer is not words. It arrives as a structured signal of impossible density, a thing that the analysts can tell is *meaning* the way you can tell a page is writing without being able to read it: patterned, deliberate, addressed to the ship, and utterly beyond the crew's frame to decode. Something out there heard the greeting, considered it for two generations, and has replied in a language the ship has no key to. The council must decide what to do with an answer it cannot understand.

outcomes.label: Devote the ship to decoding it
outcomes.description: Turn the ship's whole intellectual weight on the signal — the archive, the analysts, a generation of scholars if that is what it takes — and try to learn the language the watchers answered in, whatever it does to the crew that learns it.
outcomes.log: The ship bent its mind wholly to the watchers' reply, and the long work of learning an alien language changed the ones who learned it — a crew reaching across an unbridgeable gap, and drifting, in the reaching, further from the people who had sent the first hello.

outcomes.label: Log it, unread
outcomes.description: Some conversations are not the ship's to finish. Record the answer in full, preserve it for descendants who may one day hold the key, and let the greeting and its reply stand as a thing begun, not a thing understood.
outcomes.log: The ship logged the watchers' answer whole and unread, a reply it could neither ignore nor understand, and carried it on — a conversation across generations left, for now, at hello and an incomprehensible reply, for grandchildren to maybe one day finish.

title: The Slow Pulse
description: The deep array catches a thing that should not be there: a pulse from far ahead, faint past the edge of noise, too weak to place and far too regular to be a star. Once every eleven hours and a little, patient as a dripping tap, it has been arriving — the analysts think — for longer than anyone thought to look. It is not natural. It may not be anything. To know, the ship would have to turn its whole scientific mind to it for a season.

outcomes.label: Turn the archive to it
outcomes.description: Give the anomaly the ship's full attention — the archivists, the array, a season of computing that could have run a hundred other things. Chase the pulse to its source, whatever the cost in effort and diverted power.
outcomes.log: The ship turned its whole scientific mind to the pulse — the archive, the array, a season of borrowed power — and settled in to chase a whisper across the dark to whatever was patient enough to be sending it.

outcomes.label: Log it and hold course
outcomes.description: Note the pulse, file it, and keep the schedule. The ship is not a laboratory adrift; it has a charter and a heading, and a curiosity that cannot pay its way waits.
outcomes.log: The pulse went into the log with a date and a frequency and no answer, and the ship held its heading. Some questions are noted and left for a generation with time to spare.

outcomes.label: Answer it in kind
outcomes.description: Do not merely listen — reply. Fold the ship's own signature into a pulse of the same slow cadence and send it ahead, and see whether the dark, given a hello, gives one back.
outcomes.log: The ship answered the pulse with a pulse of its own and set the watch to listening down the years — a hello flung into the dark on the chance that patience there was a kind of mind, and a small unease settling over a crew that had, perhaps, just told something where they were.

complications.description_add: The trouble is the archive itself: run down, half its analytic craft lost to the generations, it is no longer sure it could make sense of the pulse even given the season — the ship has the will to chase the whisper but perhaps no longer the ear.
complications.log: The season of study foundered on a decayed archive — the methods half-remembered, the old instruments un-serviced — and the ship learned less of the pulse than it had hoped, a mind reaching for a faint thing with a hand gone unsteady.

complications.description_add: And on a ship drifted this far from the founders, the pulse is not, to most aboard, a signal at all — it is a voice, a summons, the dark clearing its throat, and the analysts' careful talk of frequency and source falls on a crew that has already decided what it means.
complications.log: The ship's drifted generations did not wait for the analysis: the pulse was a voice, everyone agreed, and the only question was what it wanted — and the archivists' patient work went out over a crew who had already made the slow tap into scripture.

title: What the Pulse Was
description: A generation ago the ship spent a season chasing a slow pulse from the deep. The work has borne its fruit at last, and the archive lays it before the council: the pulse is a beacon, ancient and automatic, marking — the mathematics is not subtle — a grave. A ship like this one, or unlike it, died out here a long time ago, and set its instruments to say so forever, to no one. The ship has caught a message that was only ever a headstone.

outcomes.label: Chart it and carry the warning
outcomes.description: Record the dead ship's marker in full and add its lesson to the ship's own deep memory: here is a way the dark kills, and here is a people who wanted, above all, to be remembered having tried.
outcomes.log: The ship charted the beacon and the grave it marked, and wrote the dead into its own archive — a warning and a kinship both, one voyage saluting another across the failure that had ended it, and resolving, quietly, to be the one that did not need a headstone.

outcomes.label: Let the dead keep their own counsel
outcomes.description: A headstone is not an invitation. Log the finding, dim the array, and leave the beacon its lonely watch — the ship has enough of its own mortality to think on without adopting a stranger's.
outcomes.log: The ship logged the grave and looked away, leaving the ancient beacon to mark its dead for whatever else might one day pass — a mercy of sorts, and a refusal to carry one more weight of the dark's making.

complications.description_add: The Ascension Circle, who run the ship, will not have it called merely a grave: the beacon is a mind still running, they argue, a survival of a kind, and the dead crew's long-dead choice to persist as a signal is precisely the immortality they themselves reach for. They want it answered as kin, not charted as a warning.
complications.log: The Circle would not let the beacon be a mere headstone — it was, they held, a people who had beaten death by becoming a message, and the ship argued for a generation about whether it had found a grave or an ancestor.

title: The Ringing Hull
description: The ship has sailed into a region of space that will not let it be quiet. Something out there — a tide of gravity, a field no instrument names cleanly — has found the hull's own note and will not stop sounding it, so that the whole vessel rings, faint and endless, like a struck bell that never fully dies. It is not dangerous, the engineers say. It is only that no one can sleep, and the ship has begun, very softly, to hum.

outcomes.label: Damp it out
outcomes.description: Set the engineers to killing the resonance — bracing, counter-vibration, whatever the bay can rig — and buy the ship back its silence at the cost of the effort and the parts.
outcomes.log: The engineers went to war on the note, and after a long fortnight of bracing and counter-signal the great bell of the hull fell quiet at last. The ship slept, and thought no more of the field that had rung it.

outcomes.label: Ride it out
outcomes.description: Spend nothing. The region will pass; the note will fade when the ship clears the field. Endure the sleepless weeks and let physics do the rest.
outcomes.log: The ship chose to endure, and endured — weeks of a hull that hummed in the teeth and the inner ear, a crew short-tempered and hollow-eyed, until the field thinned and the note guttered out on its own and the silence, when it came, was the sweetest thing anyone could remember.

outcomes.label: Tune the ship to the note
outcomes.description: Do not fight the field — join it. Let the acousticians map the resonance and set the ship's own systems in sympathy with it, turning an affliction into an instrument that reads the invisible field from the inside.
outcomes.log: Rather than silence the note the ship learned to sing it — tuned its sensors to the ringing and read, in the shape of its own trembling, a map of the unseen field it swam through. The crew slept badly and learned much, and carried away a science no still ship could have found.

complications.description_add: But the engineering bay is in no state to fight a hull-wide resonance — under-tooled, under-crewed, its best rigs long since cannibalized — and what damping it can manage will be partial and hard-won, the silence bought only in patches.
complications.log: The run-down bay could only half-master the note, bracing one deck quiet while another still sang, and the ship bought its patchy silence at twice the parts and twice the grief a sound bay would have paid.

complications.description_add: And the old hands have felt this before — the same note, the same sleepless weeks, some crossings back — and the dread of it now is worse than the sound, a ship that has learned this stretch of the dark rings its bones and can only brace to be rung again.
complications.log: It was not the first time the hull had rung, and the knowing made it worse — a crew bracing for a sleeplessness it remembered in its teeth, the dark playing the same long note on the same tired bell.

title: The Cold Lantern
description: There is a light off the bow that has no business shining. The body is cold — the ship has ranged it, and it gives off no heat a dead rock shouldn't — and yet it glows, a steady lantern-yellow, from no fire and no star and no reaction anyone aboard can name. It is the wrongness of it that unsettles the watch: not a danger, not a mystery with an obvious shape, only a cold thing that shines, patiently, as the ship goes by.

outcomes.label: Slow and study it
outcomes.description: Give up some of the schedule to sit with the impossible thing — every instrument the ship has, trained on a cold light, until it gives up how it shines.
outcomes.log: The ship slowed and stared, and stared, and in the end wrestled from the cold lantern a sliver of how it did the thing it should not — not the whole answer, but enough to enrich the archive and to leave the crew with the particular joy of a question that fought back and lost a little.

outcomes.label: Note it and pass by
outcomes.description: The dark is full of things that do not add up, and a ship cannot stop for each. Log the cold lantern, mark its bearing for anyone who follows, and hold the heading.
outcomes.log: The ship marked the cold lantern's bearing and passed it by, one more wrongness filed against a day with time to wonder — a light that should not shine, shining, dwindling astern, unexplained and let be.

complications.description_add: And this is a ship that has made a name for chasing exactly such marvels — its chronicle thick with charted impossibilities — so the watch does not even pretend it will pass the cold lantern by; the only question anyone asks is how long the ship will linger.
complications.log: A ship famous for its wonders does not sail past a cold light that shines, and no one aboard expected it to — the lantern was studied as a matter of the ship's own character, one more impossibility gathered by a vessel that had made gathering them its name.
